question_text: What is your current academic stage?

options.text: Grade 8 or 9 — Still exploring, no stream yet
Grade 10 — Choosing my stream soon
Grade 11 or 12 — Science stream
Grade 11 or 12 — Commerce stream
Grade 11 or 12 — Arts / Humanities stream
College — Year 1 or 2

question_text: Have you already decided what you want to do after school/college?

options.text: Yes, I have a clear idea
I have a few options in mind but I'm not sure
Not at all — that's why I'm here
I thought I knew, but I want to explore more

question_text: How much pressure are you under right now to decide your career?

options.text: A lot — family expects a decision soon
Some — it comes up occasionally
Not much — I have time to figure it out
Very high — I feel lost and stressed about it

section_label: What You Do With Free Time
question_text: It's a free Saturday with zero obligations — no school, no family commitments. What are you most likely doing?
instruction: Pick the one that feels most like you.

options.text: Drawing, designing, making something visual or creative
Exploring technology — coding, building apps, tinkering with electronics
Reading, researching, or going deep on something I'm curious about
Spending time with friends, having long conversations, or helping someone
Playing sport, going outdoors, working out, or doing something physical
Planning a project, thinking about a business idea, or organising something

section_label: What You Do With Free Time
question_text: When you watch YouTube or scroll through content, what kind of videos do you keep coming back to?

options.text: Behind-the-scenes of films, animation, design, or art
Science experiments, tech reviews, or 'how things work'
Finance, startups, business stories, or investing
Sports highlights, fitness routines, or outdoor adventures
Documentaries about people, society, psychology, or history
Game walkthroughs, programming tutorials, or gadget builds
Climate, environment, social justice, or news
Music, performance, storytelling, or personal vlogs

section_label: What You Do With Free Time
question_text: Imagine you have one month of holidays with a small budget. Which project would excite you the most?

options.text: Creating a short film, comic strip, or illustrated story
Building a working gadget, app, or automation for something at home
Researching a topic deeply and writing a detailed report or blog
Teaching or tutoring younger kids in your neighbourhood
Organising a trip or sports event with friends
Starting a small side hustle — selling something, creating a service

question_text: You notice that the canteen at your school wastes a lot of food every day. What's your first instinct?

options.text: Design a better menu or ordering system so less food is prepared unnecessarily
Build a digital system that tracks orders and predicts demand
Start a campaign, talk to the canteen staff, rally students to change behaviour
Collect data on what food gets wasted, analyse patterns, and present findings
Organise a system to donate leftover food to nearby shelters or students in need
Research how other schools globally solved the same problem and adapt the best solution

question_text: Your school is organising a science fair. Which project would you most want to work on?

options.text: A model of a renewable energy system that actually generates power
An infographic installation visualising how the human brain processes emotions
A robot that can help differently-abled students navigate the school
A data study comparing how students' sleep patterns affect academic performance
An interview-based documentary about local farmers affected by climate change
A scaled physical model of a better-designed school building with green features

question_text: You're given a big, unstructured problem with no clear answer. How do you usually approach it?

options.text: I break it into small parts, list everything I need to understand, then research each
I sketch ideas, make diagrams, or draw out what the solution might look like
I talk it through with someone — bouncing ideas helps me think clearly
I jump in and start experimenting — trial and error works better for me than planning
I create a plan, assign steps, and think about resources and timeline
I look for someone who has already solved a similar problem and learn from them

question_text: You discover a new app has a terrible onboarding experience — most users drop off in the first 30 seconds. What do you want to do?

options.text: Redesign the visual flow and make it feel more intuitive and inviting
Fix the technical bugs and optimise the loading speed
Interview users to understand exactly where and why they're dropping off
Analyse the drop-off data to find the exact screen where it happens
Reimagine the whole product strategy — maybe the problem is bigger than the onboarding
Rewrite all the in-app text to be clearer and more friendly

section_label: What Kind of Work Energises You
question_text: Which of these would feel like 'a great day at work' for you?

options.text: You came up with a creative idea that people genuinely loved
You solved a complex technical or analytical problem no one else could figure out
You helped someone through a really hard moment and they felt supported
You convinced a room full of people to back your idea
You spent the day working outdoors or with your hands, and built something real
You discovered a new insight through research that nobody had noticed before

section_label: What Kind of Work Energises You
question_text: Which work environment sounds the most appealing to you?

options.text: A focused desk with a big screen — deep work, code, data, or design
In the field — visiting places, meeting people, doing things on-site
A collaborative open space — brainstorming, meetings, working with people
A lab or workshop — experiments, prototypes, and physical building
Fully remote, from anywhere — autonomy and flexibility
A studio — music, art, film, design, or performance

section_label: What Kind of Work Energises You
question_text: When working in a group, which role do you naturally gravitate toward?

options.text: The one who comes up with the big idea and keeps the vision clear
The one who figures out how to actually make it work technically
The one who communicates the idea to others and brings everyone together
The one who does the research, checks facts, and makes sure decisions are data-backed
The one who keeps the team morale high and resolves conflicts when they arise
The one who just gets things done — execution over talk

question_text: You're 30 years old, successful in your career. Which of these feels most satisfying?

options.text: People experience your creative work — art, design, film, games — and it moves them
You contributed to a scientific breakthrough that could save or improve many lives
You built a business that employs hundreds of people and solves a real problem
You helped shape the next generation — through teaching, mentoring, or public service
You worked on solving a massive societal problem — climate, inequality, healthcare
You're at the top of your field — globally recognised for your technical mastery

question_text: Which of these problems makes you most angry when you read about it?

options.text: Climate change and environmental destruction
The lack of mental health support for young people
The digital divide — millions of people without internet or technology access
The world is full of ugly, poorly designed experiences — bad apps, bad spaces
Financial inequality — a few people own most of the world's wealth
AI and automation threatening jobs without anyone preparing for it

question_text: What motivates you most in the long run?

options.text: Financial freedom — the ability to live without money being a constant worry
Recognition — being respected and known for your work
Mastery — becoming one of the best in the world at something
Impact — knowing your work genuinely made the world better
Balance — a fulfilling career that also gives you time for your personal life
Discovery — the thrill of exploring unknown territory and learning new things

question_text: You're watching the news. A city flood has displaced 10,000 families. Which of these responses most matches how you'd want to help?

options.text: Design a better early warning and evacuation system so this never happens again
Analyse the disaster data to understand what failed and write a report for policymakers
Organise a relief camp, coordinate volunteers, and make sure people get what they need
Help rebuild — physically working on infrastructure, shelters, and essential services
Use your platform to raise awareness and donations for affected families
Research climate-resilient urban planning models that could prevent this in the future

question_text: A friend comes to you struggling — they failed a big exam, their parents are upset, and they feel like a failure. What's your instinct?

options.text: Sit with them, listen fully, and make sure they feel heard before saying anything
Help them figure out exactly what went wrong and build a better study plan
Distract them with something fun and creative — help them get out of their head
Ask questions to help them think through it themselves — coach them through it
Remind them of all their strengths and encourage them to get back up quickly
Suggest a concrete next action — contact the teacher, retake the exam, use a resource

question_text: You have the chance to start a project of your choice with full support. Which one do you pick?

options.text: Build an app that solves a problem you've personally experienced
Create a documentary or short film about something you care deeply about
Run a research experiment to test a hypothesis you've always been curious about
Design and run a workshop to teach younger students a skill you love
Launch a community initiative that addresses a local environmental issue
Design a physical product — something you can hold, use, and show people

question_text: You're at a party and someone asks, 'So what are you into?' Which of these comes out of your mouth first?

options.text: I love making things — design, art, videos, whatever I can get creative with
I'm really into tech — I like understanding how things are built
I like thinking about systems and data — understanding how things actually work
I love being around people — I'm a bit obsessed with understanding what makes them tick
I'm thinking about starting something — I want to build my own thing one day
I'm really into sport / fitness / being active — it's a big part of my life

question_text: Your school gives every student a 20-minute slot to present to the whole school. What do you present?

options.text: A creative project — an artwork, a short film, a performance, or a design piece
A science or data experiment I ran myself — with real findings
A talk about a cause I care about — something the school needs to hear
A business idea or startup pitch with a prototype or plan
A documentary or interview series — stories of real people doing interesting things
A thought experiment or philosophical question — something that challenges how we think

question_text: Which subjects have you genuinely enjoyed (not just been good at, but actually liked)?

options.text: Mathematics
Physics
Biology / Life Sciences
Chemistry
Computer Science / IT
English / Literature / Languages
Art / Design / Craft
History / Geography / Social Studies
Economics / Business Studies / Accounts
Physical Education

question_text: Which of these skills feels most natural to you right now?

options.text: Writing or storytelling
Public speaking or persuading people
Working with numbers, spreadsheets, or data
Drawing, designing, or visual thinking
Listening and empathising with people
Fixing, building, or assembling physical things
Coding, technology, or figuring out software
Planning, organising, and project managing
Performing — acting, singing, dancing, presenting
Researching and synthesising complex information

question_text: How many of these careers have you heard of before?

options.text: UX Designer
Aerospace Engineer
Penetration Tester (Ethical Hacker)
Genomic Counselor
Urban Planner
Esports Coach
Climate Change Analyst

Forensic Psychologist
Marine Biologist

question_text: When you think about careers, which of these best describes your current mental model?

options.text: I think there are maybe 10–15 real career options
I know there are many careers but I can only think of 20–30
I know there's a huge range and I want to explore all of it
I already know the field I want — I just need to find my specific path within it

question_text: If you had to choose, would you rather:

options.text: Build things (products, systems, tools, structures)
Understand things (research, study, analyse, discover)
Help people (support, teach, heal, guide)
Lead things (start, grow, manage, change)
Protect things (environment, rights, health, culture)
Express things (create, perform, design, tell stories)

question_text: One last one. Read this description and pick the one that most excites you:

options.text: You wake up and spend your day building digital systems, training AI models, or solving engineering problems.
You spend your day in a studio — directing, designing, writing, or bringing creative ideas to life.
You work in a lab or hospital — researching diseases, developing drugs, or helping patients.
You sit in a room full of data and your job is to find the patterns nobody else can see.
You're in the field, in government offices, or in classrooms — working on how society functions.
You're building your own thing — a startup, a platform, a movement, a brand.
You work outdoors or in research institutions trying to fix the planet.
Your work is physical — sport, performance, hands-on craftsmanship.
